title: Connected Components in a Graph
description:
Given an undirected graph represented by a set of nodes and edges, determine the number of connected components in the graph. A connected component is a subset of the graph where there is a path between any two nodes in that subset. You need to implement a function that accepts a list of edges and returns the number of connected components.
For example, if you have nodes labeled from 0 to n-1, a list of edges will be in the form of edges = [[u1, v1], [u2, v2], ...].

examples.explanation: The nodes {0, 1, 2} form one connected component, while nodes {3, 4} form another. Hence, there are 2 connected components.
Nodes {0, 1, 2, 3} are all connected, making one component. Nodes {4, 5} are separate, resulting in another component. Thus, we have 2 connected components.
With no edges, each node (0 through 4) is its own component. Therefore, there are 5 connected components.

constraints: The number of nodes n will be in the range [1, 1000].
The edges will not contain self-loops or duplicate edges.
hints: Consider using Depth First Search (DFS) or Breadth First Search (BFS) for exploring the graph.
You might want to keep track of visited nodes to avoid counting the same component multiple times.
Think about how to represent the graph, an adjacency list may be useful.
Explore the Union-Find data structure as an alternative approach for managing connected components.
